title: Combat oracle floor.stan_id/stan_flags not cross-comparable native-vs-ares (tile id encoding differs; room+height agree)
repro: tools/movement_oracle_capture.sh --route dam_forward_stop --native-full-trace --ares-bin build/ares-movement-oracle/.../ares --no-compare --out-dir /tmp/cap; tools/compare_combat_trace.py --baseline /tmp/cap/stock_dam_forward_stop.jsonl --test /tmp/cap/native_dam_forward_stop.jsonl --align move ; floor.stan_id agrees 0/138 while stan_room+height agree 138/138

history.note: Both-sides Dam dam_forward_stop capture (FID-0032 verify). Over 138 motion-anchored frames the player floor tile agrees on stan_room (138/138) and height (138/138) but stan_id NEVER agrees (0/138): ares 405895 vs native 3211270 at anchor. Physical tile identical => this is a StandTile.id READ/encoding divergence, not tile-selection drift. NATIVE_PORT widens StandTile (pointer inflation) so the native C .id field is not the N64 24-bit packed id the ares side masks (0x00FFFFFF). stan_flags diverges on 60 frames from the same read path. Blocks F2 (FID-0013) frame-exact tile-selection verification: the field must be reconciled to a common encoding before stan_id divergences can be trusted as tile-selection findings.
